entry.title: Demo: Conversational Analytics with SAP Analytics Cloud in Business Data Cloud
entry.content:
Today’s business moves fast. And to keep up, we need to make decisions quickly and with confidence. But even in data-rich organizations, that’s not always easy, too often the insights people need are locked behind tools, dashboards, or technical expertise. Despite heavy investment in analytics, many business analysts and decision-makers still struggle to access the right data at the right time. They may know what they need, but not where to find it, or how to get it fast enough.

That’s the problem conversational analytics solve, thanks to generative AI, business analysts and all decision-makers can now ask questions in plain language. Like “Show me actuals versus forecast by region.” And instantly, they get clear, contextual insights, no delays.

Watch a demo of conversational analytics with SAC in BDC

In this video, first, we demonstrate the just ask feature for natural language queries. With the just ask NLQ feature in SAP Analytics Cloud, business analysts can ask questions in plain language.
Then we demonstrate analytical insights in the Joule AI copilot. Joule takes the NLQ experience to next level, with a real conversational analytics experience for all business users, right where they work, no switching tools.
The result is faster decisions, higher productivity, and a culture where using data becomes effortless and automatic.

Recommended Resources

Blog Post: Conversational analytics: Why rich metadata and business semantics matter more than ever

Blog Post: SAP Business Data Cloud for the Business Analyst: turning raw data to trusted and actionable insight

Hands-on: SAP Business Data Cloud basic trial

entry.title: Re: Showing Custom fields in QM Notification Item Overvie...
entry.content:
Dear @inamullah,

In Public Cloud there is no SAP-documented standard extension point to add an editable custom input field directly in the QM Notification Items Overview table for inline maintenance. SAP supported extensibility for this scenario is at item detail level for the business context Notification Item/Defect the custom field is maintained via Items tab->select item->Detail View (dialog box), which is the UI location documented by SAP for this extension. SAP also documents that custom fields can be displayed in quality notification worklist apps, including Change Quality Notification Items Worklist. However this refers to display in worklists, not to adding an inline editable field in the Items Overview table inside the notification screen

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d1e58be39d884a0dbf75a7526a9acbf4/b392cc6291ea442a8c7636f05284dd24.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d1e58be39d884a0dbf75a7526a9acbf4/c2449b9951d6448aab185c7cebd38539.html

 

Summary:
The supported standard approach is to create the custom field for Notification Item/Defect and maintain it in Item Detail View. A direct inline input field in the QM Notification Items Overview table is not covered by SAP documented standard extensibility for Public Cloud

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Unable to import AP Credit Memo with Withholding tax...
entry.content:
Hi @satinath_biswas ,

The values in the following columns are not required in your scenario: WTCode, WTAmount, and TaxableAmount.

If WTCode is set, the information from the PCH5 table of the A/P Invoices document will not be copied to the RPC5 table of the A/P Credit Memos. Additionally, when TaxableAmount and WTAmount are set, the withholding amount information for other currencies and TDS-related details will not be populated.

Therefore, please set values only for the BaseDocEntry and BaseDocType columns in the WithholdingTaxData object. The import should then proceed successfully.

Kind regards,

ANKIT CHAUHAN

SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Is it possible to connect two XI adapters to the same...
entry.content:
Hello, 

As already answered, yes, this is possible - you can use the "IS_URL" parameter to point to both PI and CPI endpoints. 

Some more details

  • SXMB_ADM Subparameter as prefix - a similar (but not 100% identical) question is discussed here. The screenshots will help to understand the configuration requirement. 
  • FAQ First Steps for Using the XI Runtime - set up instructions documented here. 
  • SAP Note 2116345 XI RUNTIME: IS_URL with several central Integration Servers, contains more details on the technical and system requirements. 

Regards
Mark

entry.title: Re: QRC1 2026 Update Memory Problem - Loading Optimizatio...
entry.content:
Hi @CJH we created a case now. thx for your support. Case 400900/2026 (P2) - SAC Loading Optimization Settings Problem. 
I identified that the issue occurs only in embed mode when pagebar=disabled.
The problem is triggered by the code in OnInitialization. This code was already present in the function before the Q1 2026 update, and at that time it did not cause any issues.

this is the code - starting your story with this (+ Viewport or Background Loading active), will lead to the error:

if (Application.getMode() !== ApplicationMode.Embed) {
    NavigationUtils.openApplication(
        Application.getInfo().id,
        [
            UrlParameter.create("mode", "embed"),
            UrlParameter.create("pageBar", "disable")
        ],
        false
    );
}

entry.title: Re: Networking Meetup Dublin - March
entry.content:
Had a really good time attending an SAP networking meetup here in Dublin 🇮🇪 yesterday..

Big Thanks to Alan for putting this together.

What I enjoyed most wasn’t just the topics, but the conversations with people who are actually working through these challenges every day.
A few things that really stayed with me:
👉 The growing importance of SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) – it’s no longer just “nice to have.” It’s becoming central to how companies connect systems, build extensions, and innovate faster.
👉 A very interesting discussion around VAT in supply chain – something that often feels like a backend topic, but actually has a huge impact on real business decisions, especially for global operations.
👉 And of course, AI. There’s a lot of buzz, but what stood out was how practical it’s becoming within SAP—helping automate processes, improve decisions, and reduce manual effort.
Overall, it felt like we’re at a point where SAP is not just about processes anymore—it’s about intelligence, integration, and real transformation.

Always great to learn, exchange ideas, and connect with the SAP community 🙌..Looking forward for next meetup.

entry.title: Frequent “insufficient privilege” (Error 258) with different GUIDs when executing procedure in SAP H
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I’m facing a recurring issue on SAP HANA and would appreciate your support.

issue description 

We are getting a runtime error (258 - insufficient privilege) multiple times per day (about 50 runtime error daily.

The error always has the same structure but with a different GUID each time.

Error Details

Error 258 has occurred on the current database connection "DEFAULT".
Database error text: insufficient privilege: Detailed info for this error can be found with guid '64B94C6F91CD624E925BF4A480F92C41' Triggering SQL statement: "EXECUTE PROCEDURE "SIM_SIMSESSION_GET_ACTIVE""

Observations

  • The issue is happening frequently and repeatedly
  • The GUID changes every time

  • The failing statement is always:

    EXECUTE PROCEDURE "SIM_SIMSESSION_GET_ACTIVE"

entry.title: Automatic Segment Derivation from Cost Center in Journal Entries (SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud)
entry.content:
I’m working on a requirement in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud where I need to implement logic for automatic segment derivation during journal entry postings.

Requirement:

  • A new segment has been created in the system.
  • Whenever an expense is posted to a specific cost center, the system should automatically derive and populate the corresponding segment in the journal entry line item.

Current Situation:

  • The segment is already created and available.
  • Cost centers are properly maintained.
  • However, the segment is not being automatically derived during postings.

My Question:
What is the recommended approach in S/4HANA Public Cloud to achieve this? Specifically:

  • Can this be handled using configuration such as Manage Substitutions/Validations or SSCUI settings, or is a custom logic (e.g., BAdI for coding block derivation) required?
  • How can I link the cost center to the segment so that it derives automatically during posting?
  • Are there any standard apps or configuration steps that need to be maintained for this scenario?

If anyone has implemented a similar requirement in the Public Cloud environment, I would really appreciate your guidance or best practices.

Thanks in advance!

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: Exploring Middleware Options for SAP Public Cloud to...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question.

I have asked our internal chatbot for support. Here's the response, which I hope you find useful. Please check whether it helps you solve the issue:

This is a substantive integration challenge. Here's a comprehensive evaluation of alternatives to SEEBURGER:

1. SAP-Native Solutions

SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration)

Strengths:

  • Native S/4HANA Public Cloud integration
  • Pre-built adapters and content packages
  • Strong monitoring via Integration Advisor and runtime dashboards
  • Supports custom OFTP2 protocols via scripting
  • Native API-based connectivity
  • Compliance with SAP security standards

Limitations:

  • OFTP2 not natively built-in (requires custom development or scripting)
  • Additional licensing costs
  • Steeper learning curve for OFTP2 protocol specifics
  • May require SAP Custom Code Modernization (CCM) for 35 interfaces

Cost Profile: License-based (per number of interfaces/messages)

SAP Process Orchestration (Deprecated Path)

Note: Transitioning to SAP Integration Suite; not recommended for new deployments

2. Third-Party EDI/OFTP2 Specialists

MuleSoft (Salesforce)

Strengths:

  • Robust OFTP2 connector available via MuleSoft Exchange
  • Excellent for multi-protocol environments
  • Strong error handling and retry mechanisms
  • API-first architecture
  • Comprehensive monitoring/observability

Considerations:

  • Licensing model can be expensive at scale (35 interfaces)
  • Requires Salesforce integration cloud subscription
  • Not SAP-specific (may require additional customization)

IBM Sterling B2B Integrator

Strengths:

  • Enterprise-grade OFTP2 support
  • Mature platform with extensive OFTP2 compliance testing
  • Excellent for high-volume, mission-critical scenarios
  • Strong audit and compliance features
  • Established in European regulatory environments

Limitations:

  • Licensing typically higher than alternatives
  • Heavier infrastructure footprint
  • Learning curve for new teams

Best For: Large organizations with existing IBM relationships or complex B2B requirements

Boomi (Dell)

Strengths:

  • Cloud-native integration platform
  • OFTP2 connector available
  • Lower learning curve than some competitors
  • Strong HIPAA/compliance certifications
  • Flexible per-interface pricing

Considerations:

  • OFTP2 implementation may require custom development
  • Medium-tier monitoring capabilities
  • Less SAP-optimized than native solutions

3. Open-Source / Lighter-Weight Solutions

Apache NiFi + Custom OFTP2 Processor

Strengths:

  • Zero licensing costs
  • Highly flexible and scalable
  • Excellent for 35+ parallel interfaces
  • Strong community support
  • Fine-grained control over data flow

Limitations:

  • Requires internal development resources
  • OFTP2 support via custom implementation
  • Ongoing maintenance responsibility
  • Less out-of-box monitoring

Recommendation: Only viable if you have strong integration development capabilities

Open-Source OFTP2 Libraries

  • nekoX/oftp2 (Python-based)
  • Mendelson OFTP2 (Java-based, partial open-source)

Use Case: Building a lightweight custom integration layer

4. Comparative Matrix

Criterion SAP Integration Suite MuleSoft Boomi IBM Sterling SEEBURGER

OFTP2 Native SupportCustom only✓ Connector✓ Connector✓ Native✓ Native
SAP Cloud Integration✓✓✓ Native✓✓ Via APIs✓✓ Via APIs✓✓✓✓
Cost for 35 InterfacesMedium-HighHighMediumHighMedium
Monitoring/ObservabilityExcellentExcellentGoodExcellentExcellent
ScalabilityVery GoodExcellentVery GoodVery GoodVery Good
Learning CurveMediumMedium-HighLowHighMedium
European Compliance✓✓✓✓✓✓✓
Time to Production3-4 months2-3 months2-3 months4-5 months1-2 months

5. Recommended Evaluation Path

For Your Scenario (35 interfaces, Europe-based):

Priority 1: SAP Integration Suite

Rationale:
- Already integrated with your S/4HANA Public Cloud
- Reduces vendor lock-in
- Long-term strategic alignment
- OFTP2 can be handled via custom adapters or pre-built integrations

Action: Pilot 5-10 interfaces to validate feasibility
Timeline: 2-3 months

Priority 2: MuleSoft

Rationale:
- If you need out-of-box OFTP2 without custom development
- Strong monitoring capabilities
- Can run in parallel with SAP systems

Action: Request proof-of-concept for 3 interfaces
Timeline: 1 month

Priority 3: Boomi

Rationale:
- Cost-effective middle ground
- Good for teams new to OFTP2 integration
- Lower implementation risk

Action: Evaluate if SAP Integration Suite needs are complex

6. Implementation Considerations

Architecture Pattern (Recommended)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud 
    ↓
SAP Integration Suite (or alternative middleware)
    ↓
OFTP2 Protocol Handler (native or custom)
    ↓
OFTP2 Partner Network (SEEBURGER or direct)

Key Checkpoints for Selection:

  1. Protocol Compliance: Verify OFTP2 v2.0 or v3.0 support (OFTP2 versioning varies)
  2. High Availability: Ensure 99.9%+ SLA for mission-critical interfaces
  3. Batch Processing: Confirm support for large file transfers (common in B2B EDI)
  4. Security: TLS 1.2+, certificate management, audit trails
  5. Error Recovery: Automatic retry, manual intervention capabilities
  6. Monitoring: Real-time dashboards, alerting, integration with your ITSM

7. Cost Optimization Strategies

  • Interface Consolidation: Consider reducing 35 interfaces to 15-20 logical flows
  • Hybrid Approach: Keep 10-15 critical interfaces on SEEBURGER (for stability) while piloting alternatives
  • Open-Source Gateway: Use lightweight open-source OFTP2 gateway for non-mission-critical flows
  • Negotiate SEEBURGER: If staying with SEEBURGER, leverage this evaluation to negotiate better terms

8. Final Recommendation

Recommended Path:

  1. Immediate (Month 1): Conduct SAP Integration Suite PoC for 3-5 interfaces
  2. Parallel (Month 1): Request MuleSoft evaluation for comparative analysis
  3. Months 2-3: Decision gate—choose primary platform and migrate 50% of interfaces
  4. Months 4-6: Complete migration with parallel run of SEEBURGER for safety

This phased approach minimizes disruption while maintaining vendor flexibility.

Kind regards, 
Henrike

entry.title: Re: Understanding the Belgium B2B e-Invoicing Mandate and the Plant Abroad Scenario
entry.content:
Hi,

Two problems with that post and solution :

1) The scope of the B2B e-invoicing mandate in Belgium, applicable as of January 1st 2026, refers to B2B transactions between VAT-registered enterprises established in Belgium.  This means that non-established entities who are registered for VAT purposes in Belgium will not have to comply with the e-invoicing mandate.

2) Belgian PEPPOL Authorities requirements : "The Peppol SP must ensure that any Belgian Peppol Participant they service is identified on Peppol using its enterprise number, the identification scheme of the Belgian Crossroad bank of Enterprises(ICD: 0208)". (source : https://peppol.org/wp-content/uploads/2022/08/Belgium-Peppol-Authority-Specific-Requirements.pdf)

Also, customers may not want to send an invoice without the name and adress of their billing company code. Thus, at our customers, under PartyTaxSchme structuren, we only "swap" the VAT nb (for TaxScheme value VAT) with the one of the Plant abroad and kept everything else the same. Which works fine. Also, one of our customers wanted to display both VAT nb (the one from company code and the one from plant abroad). We thus used tax scheme VAT for VAT nb from Plant abroad (as explained) and used Tax scheme TAX (non_vat) for Vat nb from company code (it is the only PEPPOL structure with 0-2 cardinality). Which works fine too.

Kind regards,

Jerome

entry.title: Re: API Journal Entry-Clearing (asynchronous) the interfa...
entry.content:
Hello,

Thank you very much for your question. I asked our internal chatbot for support and hope that you find the response useful. Please check whether the AI-generated information helps you solve your issue.

Kind regards, Henrike

For more information, see Journal Entry – Clearing (Asynchronous).

Based on your three error scenarios, here are the solutions:

Situation 1 & 2: "The communication protocol does not exist"

This error occurs when the logical port or service ID is misconfigured.

Solution:

  1. Verify the correct Service ID in transaction SOAMANAGER:

    • Go to SOAMANAGER → Select the correct system
    • Check available Web Service definitions
    • The correct service ID should be something like: JournalEntryBulkClearingRequest_In (case-sensitive)

  2. Check Logical Port Configuration:

    Transaction: SOAMANAGER
→ Web Service Configuration
→ Logical Ports
→ Verify port exists and is active

  3. Correct Code Implementation:

    DATA: lo_client TYPE REF TO cl_soap_client.

TRY.
    CALL METHOD cl_soap_client=>create
      EXPORTING
        logical_port_name = 'YOUR_LOGICAL_PORT_NAME'
      IMPORTING
        client = lo_client.

    "Set up request
    lo_client->request = ...
    
    CALL METHOD lo_client->invoke.
    
CATCH cx_soap_fault INTO DATA(ls_fault).
    MESSAGE e000(SO) WITH ls_fault->get_text( ).
ENDTRY.

Situation 3: "Logical port does not belong to consumer agent"

This error means the logical port is not assigned to your consumer proxy.

Solution:

  1. Verify Consumer Proxy Configuration:

    Transaction: SOAMANAGER
→ Web Service Consumer
→ Select correct service/proxy
→ Check "Logical Port Assignment"

  2. Correct Service ID Format:

    • Use: co_fins_journal_entry_bulk_cle_srp (remove extra characters)
    • Or check the exact name in: TRANSACTION SE80 → Service Implementation

  3. Proper Code with Error Handling:

    DATA: lo_proxy TYPE REF TO z_journal_entry_proxy.  "Your proxy class

TRY.
    CREATE OBJECT lo_proxy
      EXPORTING
        logical_port_name = 'JOURNAL_ENTRY_PORT'.
    
    CALL METHOD lo_proxy->journal_entry_bulk_clearing_request
      EXPORTING
        request = ls_request_data
      IMPORTING
        response = ls_response_data.

CATCH cx_ai_system_fault INTO DATA(ls_sysfault).
    MESSAGE e000(SO) WITH ls_sysfault->get_text( ).

ENDTRY.

Recommended Troubleshooting Steps:

  1. List all available logical ports:

    CALL FUNCTION 'SOAMANAGER_GET_LOGICAL_PORTS'
  IMPORTING
    lt_ports = DATA(lt_ports).

  2. Verify Web Service is active:

    • SOAMANAGER → Check activation status
    • Ensure service is in "Active" state

  3. Check Consumer Proxy Namespace:

    • Transaction SE80 → Select your proxy
    • Verify all configurations are saved

  4. Test with Transaction SOAMANAGER directly before coding

Additional Notes:

  • Is this S/4HANA or ECC? (affects service naming)
  • Verify user authorization for Web Service consumption
  • Check if the service requires SSL/TLS (protocol settings)

Would you like help with any specific step or the exact code for your scenario?

 
 
 

Can you please help this SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition partner? Calling SOAP Journal Entry-Clearing (asynchronous) API, how to pass in parameters in ABAP Development Tools, and the interface call failed? The call attempts the following three ways, all of which report errors, as follows: Situation 1: Use standard SAP_COM_0002, and use service _ id: journalentryBulkclearingrequest _ in. Error in calling asynchronous SOAP interface: The communication protocol does not exist.

1-1 1-1 1-2 1-2 Situation 2: Use standard SAP_COM_0002, and use service _ id: journalentryburkclearningquestions _ webi. Error in calling asynchronous SOAP interface: The communication protocol does not exist.

图片2-1.png图片2-2.pngSituation 3： Use standard SAP_COM_0002 and service _ id: co _ fins _ journal _ entry _ bulk _ cle _ sprx. Error in calling asynchronous SOAP interface: Logical port does not belong to consumer agent.

图片3-1.png图片3-2.png

 
 
 

SOAP Journal Entry-Clearing API - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Solution

Since this is S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the issue is fundamentally different from on-premise. Here's the correct approach:

Key Issue: SAP_COM_0002 vs. Actual Communication Arrangement

In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you cannot use standard SAP_COM_0002. You must use the actual communication arrangement created in your system.

Correct Steps:

Step 1: Identify the Correct Communication Arrangement

  1. Go to SAP Fiori Launchpad → Search: "Communication Arrangements"

  2. Look for arrangements related to Journal Entry clearing:

    • SAP_COM_0545 (Journal Entry Bulk Clearing - Inbound)
    • Or custom arrangement created by your system administrator

  3. Note the Service ID shown (e.g., JournalEntryBulkClearingRequest_In_V1)

Step 2: Verify Communication Settings

Fiori → Communication Arrangements
→ Find Journal Entry Clearing arrangement
→ Check:
   ✓ Status: Active
   ✓ Inbound Service: Enabled
   ✓ Protocol: REST or SOAP
   ✓ Authentication: OAuth 2.0 or Basic Auth

Step 3: Correct ABAP Code for S/4HANA Cloud

Since Journal Entry Clearing is typically asynchronous, use this approach:

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report: Call Journal Entry Bulk Clearing (Async)
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT z_journal_entry_clearing.

DATA: lv_url TYPE string.
DATA: lv_response TYPE string.
DATA: lv_request_body TYPE string.

DATA: lo_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client.
DATA: lv_status_code TYPE i.
DATA: lv_reason_phrase TYPE string.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  " 1. Build the request payload
  lv_request_body = build_journal_entry_request( ).
  
  " 2. Get the service endpoint (from Communication Arrangement)
  lv_url = 'https://[your-s4hc-system]/sap/opu/odata/sap/C_JOURNALENTRYITEM_SRV'.
  
  " 3. Create HTTP client
  TRY.
      cl_http_client=>create_by_url(
        EXPORTING
          url                = lv_url
        IMPORTING
          client             = lo_http_client
      ).
      
      " 4. Set authentication (OAuth token or Basic)
      lo_http_client->request->set_header_field(
        name  = 'Authorization'
        value = 'Bearer ' && get_oauth_token( )  "Or use Basic Auth
      ).
      
      " 5. Set Content-Type
      lo_http_client->request->set_header_field(
        name  = 'Content-Type'
        value = 'application/json'
      ).
      
      " 6. Send request
      lo_http_client->request->set_cdata( lv_request_body ).
      lo_http_client->send( ).
      
      " 7. Get response
      lv_status_code = lo_http_client->response->get_status( ).
      lv_reason_phrase = lo_http_client->response->get_reason_phrase( ).
      lv_response = lo_http_client->response->get_cdata( ).
      
      IF lv_status_code = 202 OR lv_status_code = 200.
          MESSAGE 'Request submitted successfully (Async)' TYPE 'S'.
      ELSE.
          MESSAGE 'Error: ' && lv_reason_phrase TYPE 'E'.
      ENDIF.
      
  CATCH cx_http_client_error INTO DATA(lx_error).
      MESSAGE lx_error->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
      
  ENDTRY.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form: Build Request Payload
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM build_journal_entry_request CHANGING cv_payload TYPE string.

  " Build JSON payload for Journal Entry Clearing
  " Example structure (adjust to your API):
  cv_payload = |{
    "JournalEntryBulkClearingRequest": {
      "JournalEntry": [
        {
          "CompanyCode": "1000",
          "JournalEntryNumber": "100001",
          "FiscalYear": "2024",
          "DocumentDate": "2024-01-15"
        }
      ]
    }
  }|.

ENDFORM.

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Form: Get OAuth Token
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
FORM get_oauth_token CHANGING cv_token TYPE string.

  " Implementation depends on your OAuth setup
  " Usually retrieved from Communication Arrangement credentials
  cv_token = 'YOUR_OAUTH_TOKEN_HERE'.

ENDFORM.

Why Your Previous Attempts Failed

Situation Root Cause Solution
Situations 1 & 2Wrong Service ID format; SAP_COM_0002 doesn't support Journal Entry ClearingUse actual communication arrangement ID
Situation 3co_fins_journal_entry_bulk_cle_sprx doesn't exist in Cloud EditionUse OData/REST endpoint instead

Alternative: Use OData Service (Recommended for Cloud)

S/4HANA Cloud prefers OData v4 over SOAP:

DATA: lo_client_proxy TYPE REF TO /iwbep/if_cp_client_proxy.
DATA: ls_request_uri TYPE /iwbep/if_cp_request_uri=>ty_uri.

TRY.
    lo_client_proxy = /iwbep/cl_cp_factory=>create_v4_remote_proxy(
      EXPORTING
        service_id      = 'C_JOURNALENTRYITEM_SRV'  "Actual service name
        logical_port_name = 'JOURNAL_ENTRY_CLEAR'
    ).
    
    " Create request
    DATA(lo_request) = lo_client_proxy->create_resource_for_entity_set(
      'JournalEntry'
    )->create_request_for_action(
      'JournalEntryClearingAction'
    ).
    
    " Execute
    DATA(ls_response) = lo_request->execute( ).
    
CATCH /iwbep/cx_gateway INTO DATA(lx_error).
    MESSAGE lx_error->get_longtext( ) TYPE 'E'.
ENDTRY.

entry.title: Re: How to Integrate SAP Commerce with React JS
entry.content:
Hi srinivasulu_mula,

Commerce cloud officially supports Angular (Spartacus) only and you can refer below help document for more details.
Composable storefront is a lean, Angular-based JavaScript storefront for SAP Commerce Cloud. Composable storefront talks to SAP Commerce Cloud exclusively through the Commerce REST     API. https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_COMPOSABLE_STOREFRONT/6c7b98dbe68f4a508cac17a207182f4c/c9dcbd1ca0ec4219ac3c51b29c44625e.html?locale=en-US 


And do check the below documentation for extending to your own API Implementation.

OCC API :
Get an overview of how the SAP Commerce Cloud OCC API is implemented, and learn what you need to know to extend it with your own custom API implementation.
OCC provides a consistent way for web services to communicate with the platform commerce layer. Its main purpose is to expose existing commerce layer functionality to the world of web services. OCC web services are as transparent as possible, but contain some additional elements such as the separate cache, the exception handling mechanism, the authentication framework, and the attribute mapper.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_COMMERCE_CLOUD_PUBLIC_CLOUD/3476714bba0b4cb9b3eb58c270e44439/8b422f4b86691014b579bb20d6432cde.html?locale=en-US&state=PRODUCTION&version=v2211&q=OCC+API 

Regards,

entry.title: Quality Gates: Leveraging the continuous quality check (CQC) Service Report for Project Readiness
entry.content:
Quality Gates are a core component of the SAP Activate methodology, designed to ensure projects move forward in a structured, controlled, and high-quality way. Each Quality Gate serves as a formal checkpoint at the end of a phase, confirming that the project is ready to transition to the next phase with the right foundations in place.

What brings consistency, scalability, and real insight into this process, particularly for SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors, is the continuous quality check (CQC) for implementation gates service report. Rather than relying on manual validation alone, the CQC service report enables a standardized, data-driven assessment of project readiness, ensuring alignment with SAP best practices and SAP Activate methodology.  

Within SAP Cloud ALM, the Quality Gate feature is used to:

  • Conduct the associated Quality Gate
  • Submit checklist responses for evaluation
  • Request the CQC for implementation gates service report (only for Prepare and Explore phases)

Once submitted, the CQC service report is automatically generated based on the checklist inputs. It delivers tailored recommendations specific to your project.

The results are fully integrated within SAP Cloud ALM:

  • CQC service results
  • Downloadable PDF report
  • Standalone action items

All of these are accessible within the Service pillar, providing a centralized location for managing quality insights and follow-up actions.

The CQC service report is a structured output generated as part of the Quality Gate service. It consolidates:

  • Responses to Quality Gate questions
  • Identified risks, gaps, and recommendations
  • A summarized readiness assessment

It serves as a single source of truth for the Quality Gate evaluation, particularly for the Prepare and Explore phases, and supports the decision on whether the project is ready to proceed to the next phase.

The CQC service report enhances the process by:

Standardizing the Assessment

Ensures all projects are evaluated using consistent SAP-defined criteria.

Ensuring Alignment to Best Practices

Validates that the project adheres to SAP Activate methodology and recommended implementation standards.

Identifying Risks Early

Highlights gaps and risks that could impact downtime phases if not addressed.

The report is designed to deliver value to both executive stakeholders and the project team, typically including:

  • Key Findings: A concise summary of the most important insights for stakeholders.
    • Positive Findings: Areas where SAP best practices are being successfully applied.
    • Significant Findings: Issues requiring remediation or course correction.
    • Critical Findings: Critical issues requiring remediation or course correction.
  • Action Plan: Prioritized actions with a clear priority and deadlines.
  • Assessment Results: Detailed responses to Quality Gate questions.

The Conduct Quality Gate task is no longer just a milestone, it is an opportunity to validate, align, and strengthen your project using real insights. By leveraging the CQC service report within SAP Cloud ALM, project teams can ensure they are not only completing required activities but doing so in alignment with SAP best practices. This enables a confident transition into the next phase, aligned, prepared, and set up for success.

entry.title: How to: Use OpenText Core Archive for Document Storage/Archiving from SAP BTP / Build?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

We are designing a solution on SAP BTP using SAP Build / CAP and SAP Build Process Automation and would like to use OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions as our document storage/archiving platform – without involving an SAP ECC/S/4HANA backend for the document upload.

Scenario:

  • We have a SAP Fiori / CAP front-end application with a file upload (PDF) functionality deployed in Cloud Foundry / BTP.
  • Uploaded PDFs should:
    1. Be stored/archived centrally in OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions.
    2. Be retrievable later in SAP Build Process Automation to show the document (PDF) in an approval form for review/approval.
  • We want to call OpenText directly from BTP, not via ECC/S/4HANA, and then access those documents via APIs from workflows in SAP Build Process Automation.

Questions:

  • OpenText Core Archive APIs from BTP

    • Does OpenText Core Archive for SAP Solutions expose REST APIs that can be called directly from BTP (CAP/Node/Java) for:
      • Creating (uploading) documents (PDF) with metadata?
      • Retrieving documents (either as binary or via a time-limited URL)?
  • Or is there a more “standard” pattern for using Core Archive as a central document store from BTP without ECC/S/4?
  • Are there any official guidelines or recommendations from SAP/OpenText for this type of BTP ↔ Core Archive integration?
  • Even if examples are for Extended ECM or other OpenText products, any patterns applicable to Core Archive would be helpful.

Any inputs, experiences, example architectures, or links to documentation/blogs are highly appreciated.

Thanks! in Advance.

BR,
Rakesh

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA: Data model change for BOM and Routing validity
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA introduced a new field into the BOM tables, in order to support a better performance during the BOM Explosion. A field called VALID_TO has been introduced in the BOM Header table STPO and in the BOM Item level table STKO, and they were introduced to minimize the complex calculation required to determine the BOM header and component validity, especially when the BOM is changed with a change number.

 A correct value for those fields is required for the correct BOM explosion and the calculation of the validity date in the following scenarios:

  • BOM transactions that were optimized for a better performance on HANA (CS11H / CS12H / CS13H)
  • Fiori app Maintain bill of material (F1813)
  • API API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV
  • MRP Live
  • Report CS_BOM_PRODVER_MIGRATION02

If those fields are empty, for example, MRP Live will not be able to correctly calculate the validity of BOMs and BOM components that were changed with a change number, as explained in SAP KBA 2611243, and the planning results may be incorrect.  

The routing tables were also changed, and fields VALID_TO and LOEKZ_INHERITED were added to the database tables, PLKO, PLFL, PLPO, PLAS, PLMZ, PLFH, PLMK, PLMW and MAPL.

This blog explains what can be done to avoid this issue when migrating to SAP S/4HANA.

Determine BOM Explosion Mode in Customizing

A new customizing setting has been introduced in SAP S/4HANA, in order to determine the BOM Explosion Mode. SAP S/4HANA should be able to correctly determine the values for those fields, as long as the correct value is set for Determine Explosion Mode for BOM in HANA system. We can access this customizing activity in the SPRO path Production - Basic data - Bill of material - Control data for bill of material - Determine Explosion Mode, as shown in the figure below:

Figure 1: Determine Explosion Mode customizing.

In this customizing activity, we need to and an entry for the BOM category 'Enable BOM Validity Calculation' and choose the option 'Enable BOM Validity Calculation', as shown in the following Figure:

Figure 2: Enable BOM validity calculation


This customizing setting will ensure that the BOM validity is correctly updated in STKO and STPO, whenever the BOM is changed with a change number, and SAP KBA 2516380 provides more information about this customizing step.

Update the validity in existing BOMs

The customizing change mentioned above will take care of newly created BOMs, but those fields will not be updated for BOMs that were previously created or changed with change numbers. In order to update those fields, report CS_BOM_VALID_TO_CALC should be executed in the system.

This report should be executed twice. The first time, with the flag STKO (Validity for BOM Header) set, as shown in the figure below:

Figure 3: Validity for BOM Header

And the second time with the flag STPO (Validity for BOM Item) marked, as shown below:

Figure 4: Validity for BOM Item

Note: We can control the number of parallel processes to be used in section Control Parameters of the report. System will propose a number of processes, but we can reduce, to limit system resource consumption, or increase to improve the performance. The system admin should be able to suggest an optimal number of processes to be used. 

The can also be executed for specific materials, and if the flag Validity for all BOMs is marked is set, the report will show input fields for the material and the plant.

Update the validity of existing Routings

In order to ensure that the routing operations will be correctly read by relevant applications, fields VALID_TO and LOEKZ_INHERITED should be populated into the database tables, PLKO, PLFL, PLPO, PLAS, PLMZ, PLFH, PLMK, PLMW and MAPL. Report CP_ADAPT_TASKLIST_TO_CDSVIEW should be executed in the SAP S/4HANA to populate those fields. 

Figure 5: Report CP_ADAPT_TASKLIST_TO_CDSVIEW

The Information button in the report provides the following information, which explains how each field should be used:

Conclusion

Running those reports in the system is a one-time activity, and once the customizing activity has been completed, all the required fields should be populated and the newly created BOMs and Routings should have the correct values in the tables, if the customizing setting has been correctly updated. 

 

Additional Information:

3058214- BOM valid to date inconsistency in t-code CS03 and table STKO/STPO
3691769 - Error when changing BOM with new ECN in Fiori APP F1813. 
3688932 - Manage Multilevel Bill of Material Fiori app shows several occurrences of single material
3664897 - API_BILL_OF_MATERIAL_SRV_0002 is not consistently returning "ValidityEndDate"
3619956 - Material Where-Used List in BOMs (F2214) App shows different result as classic t-code CS15/CS15M
2543040 - CS11H: Error CS_BOM_HANA001 Parameter Combination is not valid
2611243 - T-code MD01N doesn't consider BOM changes made with change numbers
3305612 - Report CS_BOM_PRODVER_MIGRATION02 cannot select specific BOM/routing
3266015 -  Routing valid to date inconsistency in t-code CA03 and table PLKO/PLPO

 

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA Regional Implementation Group

entry.title: Incomplete Results in App Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment
entry.content:
In this blog post, I would like to share an issue we encountered in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud where several users mentioned missing Purchase Orders and incomplete results in the App Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment. This may help others facing similar inconsistencies in text determination.

 

Issue:

When using the App Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment, certain Purchase Orders / Purchase Order Items can not be found.

 

Root Cause:

According to Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment | SAP Help Portal, only purchase order items that are still open are displayed. Those that are completely delivered / invoiced are not displayed.

 

Solution:

If you cannot find a specific Purchase Order / Purchase Order Item, open the relevant document in the App Manage Purchase Orders and check for the indicators Is Fully Delivered and Is Invoiced. These statuses determine whether an item is considered open and therefore displayed in the reporting app.

 

Summary:

Missing items in the Purchase Order Items by Account Assignment app are often caused by item status rather than configuration inconsistencies. Checking delivery and invoice status typically resolves the issue.

entry.title: What is a Data Steward?
entry.content:
A data steward is the business-facing guardian of an organization’s data: the person who turns fragmented, messy information into reliable, understandable, and defensible assets. They operate at the intersection of business and technology, ensuring that data is fit for purpose for reporting, analytics, and AI while making it easy for people across the organization to find, interpret, and trust the numbers they use to make decisions.

 

What they do

Rather than a long list of tasks, think of a data steward’s work as three connected threads. First, they set and enforce standards: defining business terms, documenting lineage, and embedding governance so answers are reproducible and explainable. Second, they improve quality and access by profiling data, fixing or escalating issues, and curating business-ready data products that reduce manual preparation. Third, they enable people—collaborating with IT, domain experts, analysts, and governance teams to ensure the right controls, ownership, and context are in place so decisions can be defended under scrutiny.

Why it matters

Without stewardship, organizations face competing answers, wasted time reconciling datasets, and increased regulatory or reputational risk. With effective stewardship, teams spend less time validating numbers and more time acting on insights. Trusted data shortens time-to-insight, reduces duplicate work, and makes advanced uses of data—like production AI—both safer and more reliable.

Common challenges

Data stewards contend with legacy debt, siloed systems, and tool sprawl that force manual stitching and reconciliation. Governance is often seen as a blocker rather than a safeguard, and many tools create visual outputs without preserving lineage or enforceable ownership. The most effective stewards focus on building governance into workflows, so documentation, monitoring, and lineage are captured automatically instead of retrofitted.

Tools and approaches that help

Practical stewardship relies on active metadata and an enterprise data catalog to make assets discoverable and transparent, a unified semantic layer so everyone shares meanings and metrics, and master data management to keep core reference data consistent. Governance capabilities that are embedded in the data lifecycle, plus automated profiling, monitoring, and AI-assisted mapping of lineage, reduce manual effort and make controls scalable. The goal is to simplify architecture rather than add yet another layer of tools.

Skills and traits

Successful data stewards combine domain knowledge, attention to detail, and technical fluency with strong communication and stakeholder influence. They translate between business questions and technical requirements, prioritize fixes based on impact, and run data literacy programs so users can interpret and defend results.

Getting started (practical first moves)

  • Inventory key sources and stakeholders and agree on a small set of core business definitions.
  • Profile data to find the highest-impact quality issues and capture lineage and ownership.
  • Put cataloging, basic monitoring, and governance rules in place, then iterate based on usage and outcomes.

In short, a data steward makes data discoverable, explainable, and defensible—embedding governance so teams can move quickly without sacrificing trust. With the right mix of tools and practices, stewardship transforms data from a liability into an asset that supports faster, safer decision-making and more reliable AI.

entry.title: Demystifying Model Context Protocol (MCP), Part 1: The Foundation
entry.content:
Introduction

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is quickly becoming the standard way for AI agents to talk to APIs and data sources. Today, many projects wire LLMs to backends with custom, one‑off glue code that is hard to reuse or share.

MCP changes this by defining a common language for tools and resources that any compatible agent can understand with the help of LLMs.

In this three‑part series:

  1. In Part-1, we will look at what MCP is, why it matters and when it is worth adopting in your landscape.
  2. In Part 2, we will build and deploy a commerce MCP server on SAP BTP and
  3. In Part 3, we will connect that MCP server to Joule Agent Builder to power real agentic scenarios.

What is MCP?

By now, everyone knows the full form of MCP - Model Context Protocol. Let us understand some more details and building blocks which makes MCP talk of the town nowadays.

Model Context Protocol (MCP) is an open standard through which AI applications connect to external enterprise systems,  APIs, databases and object stores. MCP wraps the complexity of heterogeneous systems and abstracts integrations away from the Agentic solutions so that agentic solutions can focus of AI piece and spend less time on integrations.

Metaphor:  Think of MCP as a universal electric adapter. Every country has its own outlet shape just as every system exposes its own API style. With MCP,  Agentic AI applications only needs one adapter. MCP defines a consistent way to describe tools and resources to plug into many different systems without integrating each one from scratch.

Building blocks of MCP

  1. Host Application + MCP Client: This is basically your agentic AI solution which embeds MCP client that talks to the MCP server on behalf of the LLM.  MCP client discovers available tools exposed by the MCP server and routes the LLM's request for data to the MCP server.
  2. MCP Server: MCP server is the application or a program which exposes the capabilities of your application or enterprise system to the host application through MCP standards.
  3. Tools: Tools are actions that the MCP server can perform. For example: "Get Product List", "Create Cart", "Add Product to Cart" etc. Tools gives host applications a structured set of capabilities.
  4. Transport Layer: MCP uses standard communication like STDIO for local and HTTP with Server‑Sent Events for remote servers.

The diagram below gives a overview of how MCP acts as a facade for the agentic AI solutions.

Figure-1

Now as we understand the "What", let us jump into "Why".

Why MCP matter?

In this section, I have listed down 3 main advantages MCP brings on the table

  1. Reduce custom integrations and increase reusability: Without MCP every AI application would build it's own integration and adapters to talk to heterogeneous APIs. MCP standardizes how tools and APIs are exposed.
  2. Provide access to data: LLMs require data to provide valuable outputs. Agents can provide this data to LLMs using MCP tools. This helps to increase the accuracy of the agentic solution and reduce hallucinations.
  3. Better managed security: MCP servers act like a facade to your enterprise data and resources. You can think of centralizing the authentication, authorization and audit logging for the underlying systems.
  4. Scale AI solutions: As MCP servers mature, you can add more tools which indirectly adds capabilities to your host application.

When it's worth to adopt MCP?

By now, you have got a good idea of MCP, but should we start building MCP server for every agentic solution? This section outlines some pointers - when MCP makes sense.

  1. When you need one integration used by many agents in your enterprise.
  2. When you want to access heterogeneous systems in a consistent way.
  3. When your agentic solution depends on real enterprise data
  4. When your agentic AI landscape is likely to grow and you do not want to reinvent integrations.

Brainstorming the need of MCP server is very important otherwise it can be one more additional code base for the team to maintain and scale.

Wrap-up

At this point, we have looked at 

  • What is Model Context Protocol.
  • The problem it solves in ever growing agentic world.
  • Building blocks of MCP
  • When it is worth adopting MCP in your enterprise landscape.

In Part-2 of the series, we will move from theory to code. I will build a MCP server, run it locally and then deploy it on SAP BTP and we can see how it can power real agentic use cases.

Disclaimer: This is not an official reference application or documentation. The thoughts outlined in this blog are based on my real world experience and learnings.

Feel free to “like“, “Share“, “Add a Comment” and to get more updates about my next blogs follow me!

References

  1. https://www.anthropic.com/news/model-context-protocol
  2. https://modelcontextprotocol.io/docs/learn/architecture

entry.title: Re: CBC behavior when an activated scope item is deprecat...
entry.content:
Hello @UmenoYagi,

Regarding your additional questions, I can report again from our experience with the example 1QR.

Scope Item 1QR is deprecated with 2602 as communicated in RASD:

 

We had 1QR activated before the deprecation.

1QR contains the apps Intelligent Scenario Management (F4470) and Quantity Contract Consumption - Release Amount/Target Amount (F2012) (as per Test Script found in Process Navigator 1QR - SAP for Me).

As of today, I can still access both apps in our system:

 

So, the scope item has not been deleted and is currently still active.

 

Generally, SAP advises to activate the Successor Scope Item. In this case, there is no successor available though, so we have not taken any specific actions yet.

 

Best regards,

Kerisha

entry.title: Difficulty setting up Joule in BTP trial account
entry.content:
Hi there. I'm new to SAP and trying to work out whether it can fit the use case I have in mind.

I'd like to use SAP Joule with some generic business trigger (e.g. low stock) to run a Joule Skill, one step of which will be running an action, which calls an API. I want to prove this is possible in a trial account for now.

I have set up a trial BTP account, and got as far as activating SAP Build Process Automation, and SAP Build Apps in case it is relevant, however when in the lobby, if I click create I only see 'Application', 'Automated Process', and 'Business Site'. When I created a trial directly for Build Apps I was able to see a 'Joule Agent and Skill' option (However with this account I was not able to set up destinations correctly, hence moving to the BTP trial account).

How can I enable Joule on this account so that I can create this flow where some generic event trigger a skill, calling and action, calling an API?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Resolve Overprotection
entry.content:
Hi Thomas,
The "Resolve Overprotection" app is probably not the tool to address your question. However, the basis to retrieve the availability and restrictions for this app is the API "Product Availability Info Service" (API_PRODUCT_AVAILY_INFO).
With this app you can retrieve availability snapshots.
Best regards,
Arno

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
@Shwethamani_M @jurgen_pirotte @stefan_berndt  I think this blog needs to be reviewed and checked for accuracy but also readability.

The fact that the CP check report returns simplification items as "relevant", the orange triangle, doesn't necessarily mean a potential contract violation. The CP checks are very basic and rudimentary. For example, the check for SI1: HR_OP only checks the number of entries in table PA0000 (>0) and whether the business function H4S4_1 is inactive. Any given ECC system where HCM was previously used would be this way, unless a clean up was done. H4S4_1 is inactive by default because activating it requires a license. The use of HR mini-master is allowed as per SAP note 3369920 and that includes entries in PA0000 so I would question the accuracy of the check.

My point is, customers have to look at each SI one at a time and assess whether the reported relevancy for simplification items are false-positives or actual findings. This will take time and resources. While the report is helpful, it is just the beginning.

entry.title: Re: Showing Custom fields in QM Notification Item Overvie...
entry.content:
Dear @inamullah,

In my opinion there are only two options:

  • On the same screen try Screen Personas and validate it through a proof of concept because SAP does allow adaptation of classic applications but SAP does not provide an app-specific guarantee for this particular table
  • If a functional solution is acceptable but not embedded directly into the standard view build a custom app, either on-stack or on SAP BTP

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/4fc8d03390c342da8a60f8ee387bca1a/2515e5ca2de74bc18255e5a62ecd2a0d.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/ee5f20b276104435a51945358572968d.html

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.content:

What I want to determine is, is it possible to configure the Auto Enroll from Waitlist setting in a class to DEFAULT to YES?  I realize it can be changed manually, but the ask from my Learning Specialists within my team is that it DEFAULT to a particular setting.  Is that possible?  As things are, it seems to default to NO. Can a default be set for "Auto Enroll from Waitlist"

Thanks!

entry.title: Additional Account assignment on payment document for withholding tax
entry.content:
Hi folks,

In the scenario below, a vendor has been paid who is subject to WHT. The issue is that the user requires the business area as a mandatory field on the payment document. which is not coming through, as shown in the screenshots below.

1. Screenshot of the payment document where the business area is required.

2.  Original invoice that is being paid.

3. Payment document generated by the automatic payment program

To address this issue, I have taken the following steps:

1. Created a new field status group and maintained the Business area as a required field.

2. Assigned the new field status group to the WHT GL for the company code. 

After making the changes mentioned above, I ran the automatic payment program. Although the Business area field was made required, the program still completed the payment run and did not populate the business area on the payment document, as shown in the screenshot in this question.

Can anyone please advise how I can make the Business area field required and copy the business area from the original invoice to the payment document? highly appreciate.

Regards
Faraz

entry.title: Re: Minimum Roles and Authorization for Technical user fo...
entry.content:
Set-up the role and user
 Create custom role ZCUS_DI_ABAP_REMOTE based on standard role SAP_DI_ABAP_REMOTE
 Create custom role ZCUS_DI_ABAP_USER based on standard role SAP_DI_ABAP_USER

For connection user which should be system user provide ZCUS_DI_ABAP_REMOTE, to the end user provide ZCUS_DI_ABAP_USER role.

also the user which is responsible for running the jobs in S4 Hana ON Premis also provide 

S_DMIS
MBT_PR_ARE = SLOP
MBT_PR_LEV = PACKAGE
ACTVT = 16

you will be good to go.

regards,

Zeeshan

 

Regards,

entry.title: Can we develop a custom program to execute SAPAPO/OM17 Live cache consistency check in bckground
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are currently performing the APO LiveCache consistency check manually using transaction /SAPAPO/OM17 on a daily basis after the required SYNC jobs are completed.

Our current manual process is:

  1. Verify SYNC jobs completion in SM37
  2. Run transaction /SAPAPO/OM17
  3. Execute Consistency Check LiveChache
  4. Evaluate the last background job
  5. Check inconsistencies
  6. Correct inconsistencies

Since this is a repetitive daily activity, we are exploring whether this can be automated.

We understand that /SAPAPO/OM17 is an interactive transaction, so directly background execution may not be straightforward. We also tried exploring recording options, but that does not seem reliable.

Our questions are:

  • Is it technically possible to develop a custom background for this requirement?
  • Are there any standard reports/ function modules/classes used internally by /SAPAPO/OM17 that can be called from a custom program?
  • Has anyone implemented a similar automation for OM17 or Live Cache consistency checks?
  • What would be the recommended approach: Custom wrapper program, background job chain, or any standard SAP-supported option?

Our main goal is to reduce manual effort and make the consistency check process more stable and repeatable.

Any suggestion or guidance would be very helpful.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: FuncionalLocationName field is always empty on API_FU...
entry.content:
Dear @saraserrano,

In Public Cloud an empty FunctionalLocationName in API_FUNCTIONALLOCATION is a known standard limitation and is usually related to language-dependent description handling, not to a communication error. The field is returned based on the relevant sign-on language, so if no description exists in that language, the API can return it as blank. SAP documents this in Note 3394360 and positions broader support as a product gap / feature request topic

More details below:

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3394360

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Learning Hub Subscription Purchasing Error (VAL_0101)
entry.content:
Hi,

I was in similar situation couple of years back (and last month) when i tried to buy learning hub as an individual. Since you have less than a week to keep current with your certifications, Fastest way to get subscription in by contacting one of your local SAP Learning partners , they can buy subscription with your SAP ID.  Partners usually need 2-3 working days (in India) to get your LHub activated.

If you have already raised a ticket with SAP i guess they can help you by directing you to a learning partner and expedite the purchase and activation.

Someone from SAP please take a note, I think SAP should seriously consider allowing individual users (who are not having a Job like me with no learning subscription from customer/partner) to buy LHub directly. Without this we individuals will end up lose time looking for partners and risk losing our certification validity.

Regards,

Jagan.

entry.title: Re: Automating the Extraction of SAP CI DS security audit...
entry.content:
There is a relevant update on this subject; the CI-DS server version 2603 allows a CSV download of the Security Log content via Administration tab > Security Log tab. This new feature appears on the Kyma platform only, CI-DS Orgs that have not migrated from the original Neo platform will not receive this feature until they migrate to Kyma.

This CSV download still requires an authorized user to access the UI and click a button, but it streamlines access to this security audit log significantly, it does not introduce any API to obtain the audit information.

entry.title: What’s new in Lifecycle Management for SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602 ?
entry.content:
SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602

 


This release brings powerful Lifecycle Management (LCM) enhancements that streamline how you manage, access, monitor, and secure your SAP Business One environment,
delivering greater efficiency for admins and a smoother experience for end users. 

Key highlights in FP 2602 include:
  • License consumption tracking in License Control Center
  • Certificate expiration visibility directly in System Landscape Directory
  • DATEV export capability in Web Client, powered by Job Service with the new Job Monitor app
  • Support User Session reminders in Web Client
  • Component version visibility in System Landscape Directory
  • New standalone components: Webhook Messenger and Microsoft 365 Integration Service

and more...

Watch the video below to see these updates in action:
▶️ Latest Lifecycle Management (LCM) enhancements in SAP Business One 10.0 FP 2602

 

Explore the full details in the What’s New guides:

  • What's New (SAP Business One)
  • What's New (SAP Business One, Version for HANA) 

💡Coming soon:  On my next blog I will walk you through the latest Identity and Access Management (IAM) innovations with Feature Pack 2602,  stay tuned!
